steps.text: Calculate the desired attenuator value corrected to the emission outlet pathlength.
Ensure the opacity monitoring equipment complies with the design specifications of ASTM D6216–20.
Ensure the opacity monitor complies with the manufacturer’s performance specifications of ASTM D6216–20.

constraints.text: This performance specification may involve hazardous materials, operations, and equipment.
It is the responsibility of the user to establish appropriate safety and health practices and determine the applicable regulatory limitations prior to performing this performance specification.
The CEMS calibration must not drift from the reference value of the calibration standard by more than 3 percent of the span value on either the high or low range.
The mean difference between the CEMS and reference values at all three test points must be no greater than 5 percent of span value for CO monitors and 0.5 percent for O2 monitors.
The response time for the CO or O2 monitor must not exceed 240 seconds.

steps.text: Calculate the CERMS Relative Accuracy (RA) expressed as a percentage using equation 2–6 of section 12 of PS 2.
Conduct a minimum of nine RM test runs.
Use method 29, method 30A, or method 30B in appendix A–8 to this part or ASTM Method D6784–16 as the RM for Hg concentration.
Conduct the RM test runs with paired or duplicate sampling systems and use the average of the vapor phase Hg concentrations measured by the two trains.
If the RM and CEMS measure on a different moisture basis, data derived with method 4 in appendix A–3 to this part must also be obtained during the RA test.

constraints.text: The RA of the CERMS shall be no greater than 20.0 percent in terms of the units of the emission standard.
If the average emissions for the test are less than 50 percent of the applicable emission standard, the RA of the CERMS shall be no greater than 10.0 percent.
For method 29 and ASTM D6784–16, use a minimum sample time of 2 hours and for methods 30A and 30B use a minimum sample time of 30 minutes.

steps.text: Ensure that the reference gas passes through all filters, scrubbers, conditioners, and other monitor components used during normal sampling.
Adjust the CEMS calibration whenever the daily zero (or low-level) CD or the daily high-level CD exceeds two times the limits of the applicable PS’s in appendix B.
Use equation 1–1 of this method to calculate the accuracy for the RAA.
At least once every 7 operating days, use a single mid- or high-level oxidized Hg reference gas to assess transport and measurement of oxidized mercury.
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure the O2 at the inlet and outlet of the control device.

preconditions.text: If the duct is >12 inches in diameter and the sampling port location meets the two and half-diameter criterion of section 11.1.1 of method 1 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–1, the duct may be sampled at '3-point long line'; otherwise, conduct the stratification testing and select sampling points according to section 8.1.2 of method 7E of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–4.
Measurements to determine O2 must be made at the same time as the measurements for CO concentration.

steps.text: Measure moisture content at the inlet and outlet of the control device as needed to determine CO and O2 concentrations on a dry basis.
Measurements to determine moisture content must be made at the same time and location as the measurements for CO concentration.
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure O2 at the inlet and outlet of the control device.

preconditions.text: If the duct is >12 inches in diameter and the sampling port location meets the two and half-diameter criterion of section 11.1.1 of method 1 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A, the duct may be sampled at ‘3-point long line’.

steps.text: Measure moisture content at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Determine formaldehyde or THC and O2 concentrations on a dry basis.
Ensure measurements for moisture content are made at the same time and location as the measurements for formaldehyde or THC concentration.
Using a pressure sensor with measurement sensitivity of 0.002 inch water, check gauge calibration quarterly and transducer calibration monthly.

The capture system meets the criteria in method 204 of appendix M to 40 CFR part 51 for a permanent total enclosure (PE) and directs all the exhaust gases from the enclosure to an add-on control device.
